summary.rationale: The GNB is materially clearer and more mobile-friendly: a 113px two-row cluster of 36px pills became a 65px single-row product header with a concise brand anchor, two direct 44px-or-larger destinations, semantic active state, and no overflow at the required 390px width. The result preserves the product surface while removing decorative navigation chrome.

notes: The primary navigation exposes only supported destinations.
aria-current, semantic links, document-order focus, and visible 2px outlines cover active and keyboard states.
No destructive or asynchronous behavior was introduced.
Both destinations remain immediately visible on mobile, avoiding unnecessary disclosure behind a menu.
The 390px layout stays on one row with 44px and 64px targets and no document overflow.
The old subtitle, stale destinations, and redundant pill containers are absent.
The active underline and text contrast remain consistent between Canvas and Songs.
The product-craft-workbench profile is expressed through restrained Geist typography, stable gutters, semantic neutrals, and one purposeful active indicator.
The design avoids filled chips, shadows, gradients, icons, badges, visual-noise stacking, and false spaciousness.
The header balances brand presence with working-content priority and consumes 48 fewer vertical pixels on mobile.
The final served header uses Geist / Geist Fallback and existing semantic Tailwind/shadcn tokens.
No unapproved global token, theme variable, semantic CSS class, or unrelated visual system was introduced.
Relevant Next.js, shadcn, Geist, React, and browser guidance was applied without adding a component abstraction that two direct links do not need.
